content: Spring Boot 4 + Spring Security 7 (ships with Boot 4) changes CSRF defaults: CSRF protection is now ON by default for REST API endpoints. Previously Boot 3 stateless REST APIs worked fine without explicit CSRF config. Boot 4 adds 403 on all POST/PUT/DELETE without CSRF token. Fix: explicitly add csrf.disable() in SecurityFilterChain for stateless APIs. Also: authorizeRequests() removed — must use authorizeHttpRequests(). Backend already on Boot 4.1 so this may already be handled, but worth verifying.
